5.1.1: Email ConveyancerSupportTeam@lloydsbanking.com if the seller owned the property for less than 6 months
Remortgages require minimum 6-month ownership, except in cases of inheritance confirmed in writing
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are not acceptable
Lease grants where the seller immediately acquired the freehold are treated as unacceptable sub-sales
Assignable, Fulfilment, Assisted, Assignment, Novation, and Transferable contracts are prohibited
Report any Unilateral Notices registered against the freehold title
5.2.1: Contact New Business Processing if the seller is not the registered owner
5.4.4: Contaminated land or environmental reports are not required

5.8.1: Building conversions of up to four flats are acceptable if the borrower occupies one and the owners share the freehold
5.8.5: Security involving two leasehold flats with reciprocal freehold ownership is accepted

5.9.1: Lending on commonhold property is permitted
5.10.1: Contact New Business Processing regarding use restrictions
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from immediate family members are acceptable without reporting if they are non-repayable and no interest is acquired
Accepted relatives include: blood relatives, spouses, civil partners, children, step-children, in-laws, and co-habitees
Gifts from unrelated third parties (friends, employers) are prohibited
Gifts from vendors or Buy-to-Let family vendors are not acceptable
Clear bankruptcy searches required for both borrower and donor
Deeds of Trust or Second Charges to protect gifts are not permitted
Email ConveyancerSupportTeam@lloydsbanking.com for other deposit scenarios
